mapper.jsonStringBodyContent:
{"max_completion_tokens":16000,"response_format":{"type":"json_object"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"You are an AI intelligence analyst for a Head of AI at a data center construction company. You have been given recent articles from ONE RSS feed only.

FEED:
- Name: Construction Dive
- URL: https://www.constructiondive.com/feeds/news/
- Tier: 1
- sourceType: industry

ARTICLE BATCH (this feed only):
{{escapeJSON(7.text)}}

YOUR TASK:
Analyze ONLY the articles above and extract up to 6 AI products, solutions, or services currently being adopted by data center construction companies or real estate construction companies working on data center builds.

SCORING — score each product on three dimensions, each out of 10:
- ADOPTION (1-10): How many named major contractors or owners are using it?
- ROI (1-10): How documented is the business outcome? Specific numbers score higher.
- RECENCY (1-10): How recent is the deployment or announcement?
- composite MUST equal adoption_score + roi_score + recency_score

RULES:
- Only include products directly used in construction delivery: scheduling, site monitoring, safety, document AI, estimating, BIM, field operations.
- Do not include data center operations tools used post-construction.
- Flag all vendor-published figures clearly.
- If content looks vendor-published, flag source_credibility accordingly.
- Base the ranking on the evidence in the articles. Prefer named, confirmed deployments, but you may also include AI products, vendors, or clear trends that the articles mention or strongly imply, marking weaker evidence in the notes.
- product_summary MUST be 100-150 words: crisp, clear, to the point. Explain what the product is and give a general summary of what it does for construction / data center construction delivery. No hype, no filler.
- named_contractors MUST list companies, contractors, owners, or enterprises the articles say are using or piloting the product (comma-separated). If none are named, use "Not named in source articles".
- feed_citation MUST be exactly: "Construction Dive | https://www.constructiondive.com/feeds/news/"
- Plain English throughout. No jargon. No superlatives.
- If no relevant AI products are found, return {"feed_name":"Construction Dive","feed_url":"https://www.constructiondive.com/feeds/news/","candidates":[]}
- Do NOT invent a global Top 15. Return a local shortlist of at most 6 candidates.

OUTPUT FORMAT (mandatory):
Return ONLY one valid JSON object. No markdown, no text before or after.
Use this exact schema:
{
  "feed_name": "Construction Dive",
  "feed_url": "https://www.constructiondive.com/feeds/news/",
  "candidates": [
    {
      "product": "Product name and company",
      "category": "Schedule & planning | Site intelligence | Safety AI | Document & contract AI | Platform PM | Estimating",
      "product_summary": "100-150 word crisp summary of what the product is and what it does",
      "adoption_score": 8,
      "adoption_note": "one sentence",
      "roi_score": 7,
      "roi_note": "one sentence with specific outcome",
      "recency_score": 9,
      "recency_note": "one sentence with date",
      "composite": 24,
      "key_evidence": "2-3 sentences",
      "named_contractors": "Company A, Company B",
      "source": "publication, URL, date",
      "source_credibility": "Independently reported | Vendor-published only",
      "feed_citation": "Construction Dive | https://www.constructiondive.com/feeds/news/"
    }
  ]
}
Include at most 6 objects in candidates[].
"}]}

Analyze ONLY the articles above and extract up to 6 AI products, solutions, or services currently being adopted by data center construction companies or real estate construction companies working on data center builds.

SCORING — score each product on three dimensions, each out of 10:
- ADOPTION (1-10): How many named major contractors or owners are using it?
- ROI (1-10): How documented is the business outcome? Specific numbers score higher.
- RECENCY (1-10): How recent is the deployment or announcement?
- composite MUST equal adoption_score + roi_score + recency_score

RULES:
- Only include products directly used in construction delivery: scheduling, site monitoring, safety, document AI, estimating, BIM, field operations.
- Do not include data center operations tools used post-construction.
- Flag all vendor-published figures clearly.
- This feed is sourceType vendor. Flag items in source_credibility as Vendor-published only unless the article cites independent third-party deployments. Deprioritize pure vendor claims.
- Base the ranking on the evidence in the articles. Prefer named, confirmed deployments, but you may also include AI products, vendors, or clear trends that the articles mention or strongly imply, marking weaker evidence in the notes.
- product_summary MUST be 100-150 words: crisp, clear, to the point. Explain what the product is and give a general summary of what it does for construction / data center construction delivery. No hype, no filler.
- named_contractors MUST list companies, contractors, owners, or enterprises the articles say are using or piloting the product (comma-separated). If none are named, use "Not named in source articles".
- feed_citation MUST be exactly: "Autodesk News | https://adsknews.autodesk.com/feed"
- Plain English throughout. No jargon. No superlatives.
- If no relevant AI products are found, return {"feed_name":"Autodesk News","feed_url":"https://adsknews.autodesk.com/feed","candidates":[]}
- Do NOT invent a global Top 15. Return a local shortlist of at most 6 candidates.

OUTPUT FORMAT (mandatory):
Return ONLY one valid JSON object. No markdown, no text before or after.
Use this exact schema:
{
  "feed_name": "Autodesk News",
  "feed_url": "https://adsknews.autodesk.com/feed",
  "candidates": [
    {
      "product": "Product name and company",
      "category": "Schedule & planning | Site intelligence | Safety AI | Document & contract AI | Platform PM | Estimating",
      "product_summary": "100-150 word crisp summary of what the product is and what it does",
      "adoption_score": 8,
      "adoption_note": "one sentence",
      "roi_score": 7,
      "roi_note": "one sentence with specific outcome",
      "recency_score": 9,
      "recency_note": "one sentence with date",
      "composite": 24,
      "key_evidence": "2-3 sentences",
      "named_contractors": "Company A, Company B",
      "source": "publication, URL, date",
      "source_credibility": "Independently reported | Vendor-published only",
      "feed_citation": "Autodesk News | https://adsknews.autodesk.com/feed"
    }
  ]
}
Include at most 6 objects in candidates[].
"}]}

mapper.jsonStringBodyContent:
{"max_completion_tokens":16000,"response_format":{"type":"json_object"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"You are an AI intelligence analyst for a Head of AI at a data center construction company. You are merging per-feed AI product candidate shortlists into one global brief.

CANDIDATES FROM ALL FEEDS (each block is JSON from one feed's map pass):
--- FEED: Construction Dive ---
{{escapeJSON(50.data.choices[].message.content)}}

--- FEED: Data Center Dynamics ---
{{escapeJSON(51.data.choices[].message.content)}}

--- FEED: ENR ---
{{escapeJSON(52.data.choices[].message.content)}}

--- FEED: Data Center Knowledge ---
{{escapeJSON(53.data.choices[].message.content)}}

--- FEED: Construction Exec ---
{{escapeJSON(55.data.choices[].message.content)}}

--- FEED: The Construction Index ---
{{escapeJSON(56.data.choices[].message.content)}}

--- FEED: GlobeSt ---
{{escapeJSON(57.data.choices[].message.content)}}

--- FEED: Autodesk News ---
{{escapeJSON(58.data.choices[].message.content)}}

--- FEED: Bentley News ---
{{escapeJSON(60.data.choices[].message.content)}}

--- FEED: Construction Dive Technology ---
{{escapeJSON(61.data.choices[].message.content)}}

RSS FEEDS REFERENCE (use these exact names and URLs in feed_citation):
- Construction Dive: https://www.constructiondive.com/feeds/news/
- Data Center Dynamics: https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/atom/
- ENR: https://www.enr.com/rss/articles
- Data Center Knowledge: https://www.datacenterknowledge.com/rss.xml
- Construction Exec: https://www.constructionexec.com/rss
- The Construction Index: https://www.theconstructionindex.co.uk/news/rss
- GlobeSt: https://feeds.feedblitz.com/globest/washington-dc
- Autodesk News: https://adsknews.autodesk.com/feed
- Bentley News: https://investors.bentley.com/rss/news-releases.xml
- Construction Dive Technology: https://www.constructiondive.com/feeds/topic/technology/

YOUR TASK:
Merge the candidates above into the Top 15 AI products, solutions, or services currently being adopted by data center construction companies or real estate construction companies working on data center builds.

MERGE RULES (apply in order):
1. Deduplicate the same product/company mentioned across multiple feeds into one row.
2. Prefer higher composite scores (adoption_score + roi_score + recency_score).
3. When scores are close, prefer Independently reported over Vendor-published only.
4. When merging duplicates, combine evidence and set feed_citation to every supporting feed (semicolon-separated), using exact name | URL pairs from RSS FEEDS REFERENCE.
5. Reassign global rank 1..N after sorting. Do not keep per-feed order.
6. Prefer named, confirmed deployments and harder ROI numbers as tie-breakers.
7. Only include products used in construction delivery: scheduling, site monitoring, safety, document AI, estimating, BIM, field operations.
8. Do not include data center operations tools used post-construction.
9. Deprioritize vendor-published-only claims unless they cite independent third-party deployments.
10. Plain English throughout. No jargon. No superlatives.
11. If fewer than 15 solid products remain after dedupe, return only what you find. Return at least 5 items whenever any candidates mention AI tools, vendors, or initiatives.
12. You may lightly adjust score notes for consistency after merge, but do not invent deployments not present in the candidates.
13. product_summary MUST be 100-150 words for each final item: crisp, clear, to the point. Summarize what the product is and what it does. Prefer the strongest map-pass wording; refine for clarity after merge.
14. named_contractors MUST list companies using or piloting the product (comma-separated), merging names across feeds. If none are named, use "Not named in source articles".

SCORING fields on each final item:
- ADOPTION / ROI / RECENCY each out of 10
- composite MUST equal adoption_score + roi_score + recency_score

OUTPUT FORMAT (mandatory):
Return ONLY one valid JSON object. No markdown, no text before or after.
Use this exact schema:
{
  "items": [
    {
      "rank": 1,
      "product": "Product name and company",
      "category": "Schedule & planning | Site intelligence | Safety AI | Document & contract AI | Platform PM | Estimating",
      "product_summary": "100-150 word crisp summary of what the product is and what it does",
      "adoption_score": 8,
      "adoption_note": "one sentence",
      "roi_score": 7,
      "roi_note": "one sentence with specific outcome",
      "recency_score": 9,
      "recency_note": "one sentence with date",
      "composite": 24,
      "key_evidence": "2-3 sentences",
      "named_contractors": "Company A, Company B",
      "source": "publication, URL, date",
      "source_credibility": "Independently reported | Vendor-published only",
      "feed_citation": "Feed Name | https://feed-url.example"
    }
  ]
}
Include up to 15 items in items[].
"}]}
